<!-- Topic 2: What's the Output? -->
<!-- Total slides: 6 -->

# What's the Output?

---

## Exercise 1 тАФ What does this print? {.smaller}

+ Two variables, one reference, one modification. What does each `cout` print?

::: notes
Total slides: 6
Give students 60 seconds to work this out on paper before revealing.
:::

<!-- Slide 1 -->

---

## Exercise 1 тАФ Code {.smaller}

::: {.no-copy}
```{.cpp}
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int main() {
    int x = 10;
    int& r = x;

    r = 20;
    cout << x << "\n";    // line A

    int y = 30;
    int* p = &x;
    p = &y;
    *p = 50;

    cout << x << "\n";    // line B
    cout << y << "\n";    // line C

    return 0;
}
```
:::

::: notes
Line A: 20 тАФ r is an alias for x; assigning to r changes x.
Line B: 20 тАФ p was reseated to y; x is untouched.
Line C: 50 тАФ *p = 50 modifies y through the pointer.
:::

<!-- Slide 2 -->

---

## Exercise 1 тАФ Output {.smaller}

::::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="57%"}
```{.cpp}
int x = 10;
int& r = x;

r = 20;
cout << x << "\n";    // line A

int y = 30;
int* p = &x;
p = &y;
*p = 50;

cout << x << "\n";    // line B
cout << y << "\n";    // line C
```
:::
::: {.column width="43%"}
**Line A тЖТ `20`**

`r` is an alias for `x`. Assigning to `r` is assigning to `x`.

**Line B тЖТ `20`**

`p` was reseated to `&y`. `x` is untouched.

**Line C тЖТ `50`**

`*p = 50` writes through `p` into `y`.
:::
:::::

<!-- Slide 3 -->

---

## Exercise 2 тАФ What does this print? {.smaller}

+ A reference parameter and a pointer parameter side by side. Trace each call.

<!-- Slide 4 -->

---

## Exercise 2 тАФ Code {.smaller}

::: {.no-copy}
```{.cpp}
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

void doubleRef(int& n) { n *= 2; }
void doublePtr(int* n) { *n *= 2; }

int main() {
    int a = 5;
    int b = 5;

    doubleRef(a);
    doublePtr(&b);

    cout << a << "\n";    // line A
    cout << b << "\n";    // line B

    int c = 3;
    int* p = &c;
    doublePtr(p);
    cout << c << "\n";    // line C

    return 0;
}
```
:::

::: notes
Line A: 10 тАФ doubleRef takes a reference; a is modified in place.
Line B: 10 тАФ doublePtr takes a pointer; *n *= 2 modifies b through the pointer.
Line C: 6 тАФ p holds the address of c; doublePtr(p) passes that address, *n *= 2 modifies c.
:::

<!-- Slide 5 -->

---

## Exercise 2 тАФ Output {.smaller}

::::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="57%"}
```{.cpp}
int a = 5, b = 5;

doubleRef(a);
doublePtr(&b);

cout << a << "\n";    // line A
cout << b << "\n";    // line B

int c = 3;
int* p = &c;
doublePtr(p);
cout << c << "\n";    // line C
```
:::
::: {.column width="43%"}
**Line A тЖТ `10`**

`doubleRef(a)` takes a reference тАФ `a` is doubled in place.

**Line B тЖТ `10`**

`doublePtr(&b)` dereferences тАФ `*n *= 2` writes through into `b`.

**Line C тЖТ `6`**

`p` holds `&c`. `doublePtr(p)` passes that address тАФ `c` is doubled.
:::
:::::

<!-- Slide 6 -->
